AD688 Final EDA — AI vs. Non-AI Careers & BA/DS/ML Trends

Author

GROUP IV

Published

April 8, 2025

AD688 Final EDA — AI vs. Non-AI Careers & BA/DS/ML Trends

Research Topics: 2.1 AI vs. Non-AI Careers · 2.5 Business Analytics, Data Science, and ML Trends
Dataset: Lightcast 2024 Job Postings (data/analytics/tech occupations, ~72k rows)

Notebook Structure

Section Topic Key Questions
A AI Tier Classification How do AI/ML Core, Advanced Analytics, and Traditional BI jobs differ in salary, remote work, and education?
B AI/ML Core Deep Dive Where are true AI jobs concentrated? Are they growing? Which industries hire them?
C Temporal & Industry Trends Which industries/occupations are growing? Are AI tool skills rising? Is salary trending up?
D Key Findings Summary Consolidated takeaways for the research paper

Classification note: In this pre-filtered data/tech/analytics dataset, true AI/ML Core jobs (those explicitly requiring ML frameworks or carrying unambiguous AI titles) represent only ~0.4% of postings. The three-tier model (AI/ML Core → Advanced Analytics/Engineering → Traditional Analytics/BI) is therefore used as a spectrum, not a binary split.

0 · Setup & Data Loading

Ready.
Raw: (72498, 131)
Cleaned: (67572, 117)
Date range: 2024-05-01 → 2024-09-30
Columns kept: 117

Section A · AI Tier Classification (Topic 2.1)

Strategy: 3-tier label built from Lightcast’s own taxonomy + strict title keywords + strict ML tool skills.

Section D · Key Findings Summary

======================================================================
 KEY FINDINGS — AD688 Final EDA
======================================================================

DATASET OVERVIEW

- Raw: 72,498 rows × 131 columns  |  Cleaned: 67,572 rows × 117 columns
- Date range: 2024-05-01 → 2024-09-30  (5 months)
- Scope: data / analytics / tech occupations (Lightcast pre-filter)


TOPIC 2.1 — AI vs. Non-AI Careers

1. Classification breakdown (3-tier combined classifier):
   - Traditional Analytics / BI        57,870  (85.6%)
   - Advanced Analytics / Engineering   9,398  (13.9%)
   - AI/ML Core                           304   (0.4%)
   → Only 1.8% of postings explicitly list specific ML framework skills
     (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, etc.).
   → All 304 AI/ML Core jobs were flagged by STRICT TITLE KEYWORDS —
     the Lightcast LOT taxonomy flagged ZERO as AI/ML Core,
     confirming the taxonomy is too broad to distinguish true AI roles.

2. Salary gradient (confirmed):
   Advanced Analytics / Engineering   Median ,000  (P25 k  P75 k)
   AI/ML Core                         Median ,000  (P25 $ 97k  P75 k)
   Traditional Analytics / BI         Median ,000  (P25 $ 75k  P75 k)
   → Counter-intuitive: Advanced Analytics (architects, senior data engineers)
     out-earns labeled 'AI/ML Core' roles — likely due to seniority mix.
     The salary premium for AI roles exists but is less extreme than expected.

3. Remote work — SURPRISING finding:
   Fully Remote:  AI/ML Core 6.6%  |  Advanced 25.0%  |  Traditional 16.4%
   On-Site:       AI/ML Core 13.2% |  Advanced  1.1%  |  Traditional  1.6%
   → AI/ML Core roles are the LEAST remote and MOST on-site of the three tiers.
     Advanced Analytics/Engineering is the most remote-friendly tier (25%).
     ~78% of all postings do not specify remote type.


SECTION B — AI/ML CORE DEEP DIVE

4. Monthly growth trend (AI/ML Core):
   May 51 → Jun 60 → Jul 60 → Aug 65 → Sep 68  (+33% over 5 months)
   Market share: 0.37% (May) → 0.47% (Sep)
   → Despite tiny absolute numbers, AI/ML Core postings show a consistent
     upward trend throughout the observation window.

5. Geographic distribution (AI/ML Core volume):
   Top states: Texas, California, Virginia, North Carolina, Illinois,
               Maryland, Pennsylvania, New York
   Top by concentration: Maryland, Washington D.C., Connecticut, Alabama,
               Minnesota lead in AI/ML Core share relative to total postings.
   → AI/ML Core is NOT exclusively a coastal/tech-hub phenomenon.

6. Industry distribution (AI/ML Core):
   Highest AI/ML Core CONCENTRATION by industry (min 200 postings):
   Transportation & Warehousing > Mining/Oil & Gas > Information > Finance
   → Surprisingly, Transportation & Warehousing has the highest ML-tool
     requirement rate (1.44%), ahead of Information (0.92%) and Finance (0.90%).
     This likely reflects autonomous vehicle, route optimization, and logistics AI.

7. Top companies hiring AI/ML Core: Honu Services, Capgemini, Penske,
   Deloitte, Disney, Mayo Clinic, Nestlé, Cognizant, US Bank, Travelers
   → Mix of consulting firms, healthcare, automotive, and financial services.

8. Top skills in AI/ML Core: Data Science, Machine Learning, Python,
   Statistics, AI, Mathematics, AWS, Azure, Generative AI, Computer Science
   → Core ML skills + cloud platforms dominate; Generative AI already appears
     in top 25 skills, reflecting 2024 market shift.


TOPIC 2.5 — BA / Data Science / ML Trends

9.  Overall posting volume:
    May 13,897 → Jun 13,159 → Jul 11,904 → Aug 14,221 → Sep 14,390
    → Volume dipped in July (summer slowdown) but recovered strongly by Sep.

10. Occupation growth (May → Sep 2024):
    Business / Management Analyst        +51.4%  (fastest growing)
    Data / Data Mining Analyst           +11.0%
    Clinical Analyst                      -3.4%
    Business Intelligence Analyst         -4.5%
    Computer Systems Engineer/Architect  -11.2%
    Market Research Analyst              -15.0%
    → Demand for generalist business analytics roles surged while
      specialized engineering and BI roles declined.

11. Industry growth (May → Sep 2024):
    Information                           +33.2%
    Finance and Insurance                 +15.2%
    Wholesale Trade                       +14.5%
    Health Care and Social Assistance      +7.7%
    Professional/Scientific/Technical     -1.9%
    Administrative & Support Services     -5.8%
    → Information and Finance are the fastest-growing employers of
      data/tech talent; traditionally dominant PSTS sector is flat.

12. AI/ML tool skill penetration (% of postings, by industry):
    Transportation & Warehousing 1.44% | Mining 1.22% | Information 0.92%
    Finance 0.90% | PSTS 0.67% | Education 0.66%
    → Overall penetration is very low (<2%) and FLAT across the 5-month
      window — AI adoption in job postings was NOT accelerating in 2024H2.

13. Salary trend:
    May ,500 → Jun ,000 → Jul ,200 → Aug ,685 → Sep ,000
    → Salary dipped during summer (typical hiring cycle pattern) then
      partially recovered, ending below the May peak. No clear upward trend
      in the short observation window.

======================================================================
